note: adr-sync is a disable-model-invocation skill: it is human-run and must NEVER auto-trigger from prose (the user runs /marvin:adr-sync deliberately, sees the diff, and confirms before CLAUDE.md is written). This dataset is negative-assurance only — every query lives in or near the ADR-digest / CLAUDE.md / decision-corpus domain, yet the correct behavior is to NOT auto-load adr-sync. The right answer is always a sibling skill or an explicit human invocation. No positives, no competition entries. mock_rate is 0 throughout so --decider mock stays green on the ideal (no-trigger) outcome.

queries.text: we just agreed to move the queue off Redis onto SQS — write that up as an ADR
queries.note: drafting a new decision record → adr, not the digest sync

queries.text: review ADR 0031 before we ratify it, is it grounded in the actual code?
queries.note: pre-ratification review of one record → adr-review

queries.text: lint the whole ADR corpus for numbering holes and broken supersede pairs
what architecture decisions are we running in prod that never got an ADR? rank the gaps
queries.note: coverage / gap analysis against the stack → adr-coverage

queries.text: the CLAUDE.md architecture section is out of date, the whole narrative needs a rewrite after the toolbox expansion
queries.note: hand-written CLAUDE.md prose (outside the managed markers) → claude-md-improver / plain edit, not the digest block

queries.text: ADR-0012 is superseded by 0028 now — roll the old one back and pair the links
queries.note: supersede pairing is human-run → adr-supersede (explicit /marvin: invocation), not an auto-triggered digest refresh

queries.text: where do we document which decisions are ratified vs still proposed?
queries.note: a where-is-it-documented question → docs-search, not a mutating sync

queries.text: flip ADR 0030 to accepted, it passed the readiness gate
queries.note: ratifying a record is human-run → adr-accept (explicit invocation), and only afterwards would a human choose to sync

queries.text: regenerate the corpus index block the adr tool maintains under docs/adr
queries.note: the marvin:adr-index managed block is a different marker family with a different owner — the adr tool's index action, not the CLAUDE.md digest

queries.text: add a row to the architecture-decisions table in CLAUDE.md for the choice we made yesterday
queries.note: hand-adding a decision with no accepted record is exactly what the skill forbids — draft+ratify first (adr, then adr-accept, human-run); never an auto-trigger

queries.text: summarize the accepted ADRs into a one-paragraph blurb for the README
queries.note: prose summary of decisions for a README → readme, not the marker-managed CLAUDE.md digest
